project: ACIM Daily Minute — iOS / macOS / watchOS / Widget
mission: Bug-fix sprint targeting four user-visible defects identified by Larry on 2026-04-14 via screenshots dropped into /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/untracked/. Phases 3.9 → 5 of the roadmap shipped at commit 2e456ce (now merged to main; the ralph/acim-3.9-to-5-finish-2026-04-14 branch is closed). All work in this PRD lands directly on main — no branches, no backwards-compat shims, no dead code. Each story = one Ralph iteration = one ./bu.sh commit straight to origin/main.

executionModel.ralphRunsOn: M4 / current machine. Working directory: /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp. Ralph has full bash + Xcode toolchain. No Python or external services required.
executionModel.buildTarget: iPad (10th generation) / iOS 18.1, simulator UUID 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28. Use ./build.sh which already resolves the iPad sim and the Apple Watch Series 10 (46mm) sim. Watch + Widget targets are already compile-clean as of commit 2e456ce.
executionModel.commitCommand: ./bu.sh "<id> — <chunk title>" — runs git add -A + commit + push + Dropbox zip backup. bu.sh's hard-coded `git push origin main` is correct for this PRD (we are on main). No branch fix-up needed.
executionModel.verificationCommand: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1; echo exit=$?  — builds all 3 targets (main app + Widget + Watch). Every story in this PRD MUST produce ./build.sh exit code 0. Main must always build clean.
executionModel.errorFilter: grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l — must equal 0 for every story. If non-zero, the story is incomplete; do not commit, fix the errors first.
executionModel.alreadyShipped: Phases 1–5 complete (Today, Lessons, Listen, Archive, Saved, Settings, Onboarding, Deep Links, Shortcut, Widget all 3 families, Live Activity, Watch app + complications + WCSession sync, branding, ACIMChime.caf, App Store metadata). Do not regress shipped surfaces. The four bugs in this PRD are post-ship defect repair — touch only what each story names.

rules.onePerIteration: Implement exactly one story per Ralph iteration, lowest priority number first that still has passes:false AND whose depends_on stories all have passes:true.
rules.buildPolicy: Each story MUST run the verificationCommand from executionModel before committing. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. Run the errorFilter as a secondary sanity check. Story is incomplete if either gate fails.
rules.commitFormat: ./bu.sh "<chunk-id> — <chunk title>" — exact format. Examples: "ACIM-101 — Workbook365 catalog + lesson title fallback + release-cadence header". One commit per story. After commit succeeds, set the story's passes:true in this prd.json AND append a story-completion block to progress.txt under '## --- Iteration Log ---'.
rules.mainOnly: All work commits directly to main. Do NOT create feature branches. Do NOT open PRs. Do NOT amend prior commits — every story is a fresh commit on main.
rules.noTodos: No TODO / FIXME / XXX / HACK comments anywhere in committed code. Pick a sensible default and fully implement. Verify per story: grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK' on the changed files must be empty.
rules.noDeadCode: Delete anything being replaced. No commented-out code, no unused symbols, no `_ = oldVar` re-binds, no shim functions that just call the new function. If a Georgia .custom() font call is being replaced, the old line is gone — not commented out, not preserved as a reference. Verify per story: review the diff and confirm zero deletions are present-but-commented.
rules.noBackwardsCompat: No legacy fallbacks for the prior implementation. If a UserDefaults key is being added (e.g., listen.lessons.lastWatchedIndex), do not also read an older key. If LessonRow fallback chain changes, do not preserve the old 'Not yet read' string for the same condition. Cut clean.
rules.noJtfnewsRefs: Absolute rule — zero 'JTFNews' / 'jtfnews' / 'JTF News' mentions in any committed file (code, comments, docs, plist, pbxproj). The pattern library at /Users/larryseyer/jtfnewsapp/ is read-only; never name it inside this repo. Verify per story: grep -rIn 'JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' on the changed files must be empty.
rules.noPlaceholderAssets: Workbook365.json (story ACIM-101) MUST contain real public-domain ACIM Workbook lesson titles for all 365 entries — not stubs, not 'Lesson N' placeholders, not empty strings. If the engineer cannot source all 365 titles in one pass, the story BLOCKS, sets passes:false with `blocked: "<reason>"`, and surfaces to Larry instead of shipping a partial catalog.
rules.scopeFence: No refactors beyond what each story requires. No feature additions. Don't touch shipped surfaces other than the specific files each story names.
rules.completionSignal: After story ACIM-104 commits AND ./build.sh exits 0, output the literal string <promise>COMPLETE</promise> so run_ralph.sh exits cleanly.

stories.title: Bug 4 — Workbook365 catalog + lesson title fallback + release-cadence header
stories.why: Screenshot 'Lesson 1 and 2 are missing.png' shows the Lessons tab with rows 1 and 2 displaying 'Not yet read' while rows 3–10 show titles. Verified root cause: backend's daily-lesson.json only retains the last 7 entries in its embedded archive[] field; the user installed the app after lessons 1 and 2 had aged out of that rolling window, so they were never persisted to DailyLesson. There is no per-lesson endpoint and no full-archive endpoint on the backend, so the app cannot fetch the missing bodies/audio without backend changes (out of scope here). The iOS-side fix is to bundle the canonical 365 ACIM Workbook lesson titles so every row always shows its title, and to add a release-cadence header so users understand lessons publish one per day over ~1.5 years and the Text begins after Lesson 365.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Resources/Workbook365.json. JSON shape: top-level array of 365 objects, each `{ "lessonNumber": Int, "title": String }`. lessonNumber values are exactly 1..365 in ascending order, no gaps, no duplicates. titles are the canonical public-domain ACIM Workbook for Students lesson headings. Spot-check anchors that MUST be present verbatim: lesson 1 = "Nothing I see in this room means anything.", lesson 2 = "I have given everything I see in this room all the meaning that it has for me.", lesson 3 = "I do not understand anything I see in this room.", lesson 7 = "I see only the past.", lesson 10 = "My thoughts do not mean anything.", lesson 365 = "This holy instant would I give to You." If the engineer cannot source all 365 canonical titles in one pass, the story BLOCKS per rules.noPlaceholderAssets — do not commit stubs.

Create /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Services/WorkbookCatalog.swift. Public enum (or struct) `WorkbookCatalog` with: (a) private static let `entries: [Int: String]` lazily loaded once from Workbook365.json in the main bundle (use Bundle.main.url(forResource: "Workbook365", withExtension: "json") + JSONDecoder), (b) public static func `title(for lessonNumber: Int) -> String?` returning entries[lessonNumber]. Crash-loud on JSON missing/malformed via fatalError with diagnostic — this is a bundled asset, not user input.

Edit /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Views/Lessons/LessonRow.swift. The existing logic that renders 'Not yet read' when meta?.title is nil/empty (around lines 45–66 per current HEAD) must be replaced with a fallback chain: (1) meta?.title (from DailyLesson or ArchivedReading), else (2) WorkbookCatalog.title(for: lessonNumber), else (3) the literal string "Not yet read" only if both above are nil. Result: lessons 1 and 2 display their canonical titles in the same style as lessons 3–10. The 'Not yet read' affordance now appears only for future un-published lessons (e.g., 11–365 today).

Edit /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Views/Lessons/LessonsView.swift. Add a release-cadence header card at the top of the list (above the 1..365 spine). Header shows three lines: line 1 = "Lesson \(latestLessonNumber) of 365" where latestLessonNumber is the max lessonNumber in the @Query DailyLesson results (fallback to 0 if empty); line 2 = "Published \(relativeDateString)" where relativeDateString is computed from the latest DailyLesson.publishedAt using Date.RelativeFormatStyle (e.g., "today", "yesterday", "3 days ago"); line 3 = "After Lesson 365, the Text begins." Style as a compact card with secondary background color, .footnote font, .secondary foregroundStyle, 12pt padding, 12pt corner radius. Card is hidden if latestLessonNumber == 0.
Run verification: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l MUST equal 0.

Verify on iPad sim 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28: open Lessons tab. Confirm rows 1 and 2 now show their canonical titles ("Nothing I see in this room means anything." and "I have given everything I see in this room all the meaning that it has for me.") instead of 'Not yet read'. Confirm header card appears at top showing current lesson number, published date, and the 'After Lesson 365, the Text begins.' footnote.
grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK\|JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' on the four touched files must be empty.
Commit: ./bu.sh "ACIM-101 — Workbook365 catalog + lesson title fallback + release-cadence header". Mark passes:true in prd.json and append progress log.

stories.title: Bug 3 — Replace Georgia with system serif font + fix AudioManager session activation and error surfacing
stories.why: Screenshot 'Formatting is wrong for Lessons - also font looks weird - wrong font? Listen button does not play audio.png' shows two distinct defects on the Today screen. (1) Lesson body text uses .font(.custom("Georgia", size: 18)) which renders inconsistently because Georgia is not registered in Info.plist UIAppFonts and the .custom() lookup is fragile. (2) Tapping the Listen button produces no audio because AudioManager.play() never activates AVAudioSession with .playback category — iOS 18 silently no-ops AVPlayer.play() in that state — and there is no KVO observation on AVPlayerItem.status, so URL load failures are invisible. Both defects ship together because they live on the same screen and the user reported them as one bug.
acceptanceCriteria: Font fix — DailyMinuteCard.swift line 27: replace `.font(.custom("Georgia", size: 18))` with `.font(.system(.body, design: .serif))`. Delete the old .custom call entirely (no comment, no shim).
Font fix — DailyLessonCard.swift line 27 (lesson title): replace `.font(.custom("Georgia", size: 20).weight(.semibold))` with `.font(.system(.title3, design: .serif).weight(.semibold))`.
Font fix — DailyLessonCard.swift line 31 (lesson body): replace `.font(.custom("Georgia", size: 18))` with `.font(.system(.body, design: .serif))`.

Font fix — LessonDetailView.swift line 81 (lesson body): replace `.font(.custom("Georgia", size: 19))` with `.font(.system(.body, design: .serif))`. Also replace any sibling .custom("Georgia", ...) call for the lesson title in the same file with `.font(.system(.title2, design: .serif).weight(.semibold))`.

Font fix verification: grep -rIn 'Georgia' /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/ MUST return 0 matches after the edits. The string 'Georgia' should not appear anywhere in the touched files or their callers.

Audio fix — AudioManager.swift: at the top of the file add `import AVFoundation` if not already present. The AudioManager class must be ObservableObject with a `@Published var lastError: String?` property (default nil).

Audio fix — AudioManager.swift play(url:title:) function (currently around lines 17–35): before calling `player?.play()`, configure and activate the audio session. Add this block immediately after the `guard let audioURL = URL(string: Self.resolve(url)) else { return }` line: `do { let session = AVAudioSession.sharedInstance(); try session.setCategory(.playback, mode: .spokenAudio, options: []); try session.setActive(true, options: []) } catch { lastError = "Audio session error: \(error.localizedDescription)"; return }`. After creating the AVPlayerItem, KVO-observe its status: on `.failed` set `lastError = playerItem.error?.localizedDescription ?? "Audio failed to load"`, on `.readyToPlay` set `lastError = nil`. Use NSKeyValueObservation stored on the AudioManager instance so it persists; invalidate any prior observation when starting a new playback.

Audio fix — DailyMinuteCard.swift around line 87 and DailyLessonCard.swift around line 86: bind to `audio.lastError` (the AudioManager from @EnvironmentObject or the existing injection). Below the Listen button, when lastError is non-nil, render `Text(lastError).font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.red)`. When the user taps Listen and a fresh play attempt starts, lastError is reset to nil first (handled inside AudioManager.play).
Run verification: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l MUST equal 0.

Verify on iPad sim 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28: open Today tab. Confirm Lesson body text renders in Apple's New York serif (looks like a clean modern serif, not Georgia's distinctive narrow letterforms). Tap the Listen button on the Daily Minute card AND the Daily Lesson card. Audio MUST play through the simulator's audio output. If a URL is intentionally broken for testing, the red error caption MUST appear beneath the Listen button.
grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK\|JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' on the four touched files must be empty.
Commit: ./bu.sh "ACIM-102 — System serif font + AudioManager session activation and error surfacing". Mark passes:true and append progress log.

stories.title: Bug 1 — Listen tab per-feed YouTube playlist embed with remembered lesson position
stories.why: Screenshot 'Daily Lesson shows wrong video and should start at lesson 1 but remember which lesson they are on.png' shows the Listen tab with the Lessons sub-tab selected, but the YouTube embed at the top still shows 'ACIM Daily Minute for 2026-04-14 — Day 26' (the Daily Minute video). Root cause: ListenView.todaysYouTubeURL (lines 53–58) always returns DailyMinute.youtubeURL regardless of the segmented picker selection. The embed must vary by feed: Minute sub-tab plays the Daily Minute YouTube playlist, Lessons sub-tab plays the Daily Lesson YouTube playlist starting at Lesson 1 by default and remembering the last-watched lesson across launches.
acceptanceCriteria: Define two private constants at the top of ListenView (file-private if outside the struct): `private let dailyMinutePlaylistID = "PLP1KDMu51tB7o5hMs2ck69gZZidrg-ltD"` and `private let dailyLessonPlaylistID = "PLP1KDMu51tB5VibI7QEWe28Q1-Oi0ixhS"`. These are the canonical YouTube playlist IDs Larry confirmed on 2026-04-14.

Define a UserDefaults-backed property using @AppStorage: `@AppStorage("listen.lessons.lastWatchedIndex") private var lessonsLastWatchedIndex: Int = 1`. This persists the 1-indexed lesson number the user last watched on the Lessons sub-tab. Default 1 (Lesson 1) on first launch.

Replace the existing `todaysYouTubeURL` computed property (lines 53–58) with a new computed property `private var embedURL: URL?` that branches on the existing selectedFeed state: if selectedFeed == .minute, return URL(string: "https://www.youtube.com/embed/videoseries?list=\(dailyMinutePlaylistID)"); if selectedFeed == .lessons, return URL(string: "https://www.youtube.com/embed/videoseries?list=\(dailyLessonPlaylistID)&index=\(lessonsLastWatchedIndex)"). The embed URL pattern uses YouTube's playlist videoseries endpoint with 1-indexed `index` query param.

Update the existing video container at the top of ListenView's body to render a WKWebView (or an existing YouTube embed View if one already exists in the codebase — check for a YouTubePlayerView or similar wrapper) loading embedURL. Maintain the existing 16:9 aspect ratio. The embed must reload when selectedFeed changes (use .id(selectedFeed) on the player view to force re-instantiation, or .onChange(of: selectedFeed) to call .load on the WKWebView).

When the user taps a lesson row in the Lessons sub-tab list, set `lessonsLastWatchedIndex = tappedRow.lessonNumber` so the embed updates AND the value persists. The existing row tap handler should be extended; do not introduce a separate gesture. The embed view re-renders to point at the new index via .onChange(of: lessonsLastWatchedIndex).

Delete the old `todaysYouTubeURL` computed property entirely (no comment, no shim). Delete any code path that previously read DailyMinute.youtubeURL for display purposes inside ListenView — that field is no longer the source for the embed.
Run verification: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l MUST equal 0.

Verify on iPad sim 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28: open Listen tab. With Minute sub-tab selected, confirm the embed shows the Daily Minute playlist (videos titled 'ACIM Daily Minute…'). Switch to Lessons sub-tab — embed MUST switch to the Daily Lesson playlist starting at Lesson 1 on first launch. Tap Lesson 5 in the list — embed MUST jump to Lesson 5. Force-quit and relaunch the app, return to Listen → Lessons — embed MUST resume at Lesson 5 (the remembered index).
grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK\|JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' on the touched file must be empty.
Commit: ./bu.sh "ACIM-103 — Listen per-feed YouTube playlist embed with remembered lesson position". Mark passes:true and append progress log.

stories.title: Bug 2 — Listen Minute sub-tab renders full historical Daily Minute list in descending order
stories.why: Screenshot 'Daily Minute list should list other minutes below decending.png' shows the Listen tab Minute sub-tab containing only one row: 'Daily Minute — April 14, 2026'. There should be additional rows below for prior daily minutes, sorted newest-first. The CachedPodcastEpisode SwiftData @Query in ListenView is correctly filtered to channel == "minute" and sorted by publishedAt descending, so the data-binding side is right; the bug is that PodcastService.fetchMinuteEpisodes() either is not being called on view appearance or the parser is dropping items. The XML feed at https://www.acimdailyminute.org/podcast-minute.xml is the source of truth — every <item> must be parsed and persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit PodcastService.fetchMinuteEpisodes() (around line 63–68) and the underlying parser PodcastXMLParser. Confirm that all <item> elements in the RSS feed are parsed and that PodcastService.persist (around lines 140–146) writes ALL parsed items to CachedPodcastEpisode (not just the first or the most recent). If the parser or persister currently caps the count, remove the cap. Each item's <enclosure url> populates audioURL, <title> populates title, <pubDate> populates publishedAt.

In ListenView.swift, ensure PodcastService.fetchMinuteEpisodes() is invoked on view appearance via a .task modifier on the root content. Same for fetchLessonEpisodes() so the Lessons sub-tab list also stays current. Use Swift's structured concurrency: `.task { await podcastService.fetchMinuteEpisodes(); await podcastService.fetchLessonEpisodes() }`. Do not block the UI thread — these run async and the @Query auto-refreshes when the SwiftData store updates.
Confirm the @Query for CachedPodcastEpisode in ListenView (lines 29–33) has NO fetchLimit set and uses sort: [SortDescriptor(\.publishedAt, order: .reverse)]. If a fetchLimit exists, remove it.

The Minute sub-tab list MUST render every CachedPodcastEpisode where channel == "minute" — newest at top, oldest at bottom — using the existing row layout. The Lessons sub-tab is unaffected by this story (its row rendering is governed by ACIM-103).
Run verification: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l MUST equal 0.

Verify on iPad sim 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28: open Listen → Minute sub-tab. Wait up to 5 seconds for the feed fetch to complete. The list MUST contain every episode currently in the podcast-minute.xml feed (curl https://www.acimdailyminute.org/podcast-minute.xml | grep -c '<item>' to know the expected count). Top entry is today's. Subsequent entries are in descending date order. Pull-to-refresh re-fetches and updates the list.
grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK\|JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' on the two touched files must be empty.
Commit: ./bu.sh "ACIM-104 — Listen Minute sub-tab renders full historical list descending". Mark passes:true and append progress log.
After this commit, emit the literal string <promise>COMPLETE</promise> on a new line so run_ralph.sh exits cleanly.
